titleStmt.title: The Minister-Counselor of Embassy in China ( Clark ) to the Secretary of State

div.head: The Minister-Counselor of Embassy in China ( Clark ) to the Secretary of State

opener.dateline:
Canton , April 22, 1949—3 p. m.
[Received April 22—8:20 a. m.]

div.p:
Cantel 251. Wu Tieh-cheng,Former Vice President of the Chinese Executive Yuan and Minister for Foreign Affairs. just returned from Nanking via Chikow where he saw Generalissimo,Chiang Kai-shek, who retired as President on January 21, 1949. tells me Generalissimo and Acting
PresidentGeneral Li Tsung-jen. lunching together today Hangchow to plan future. He insists Generalissimo continues support Li fullest. Among decisions to be reached Hangchow is that of selecting over-all military Commander in Chief. Wu thinks Ho Ying-chinPresident of the Chinese Executive Yuan (premier). will be selected, possibly with Pai Chung-hsiMilitary and Political Affairs Director for Central China. as deputy.
Wu says harshness Communist terms has united all forces opposing Communists and has renewed and strengthened will to resist. “We will stop them everywhere,” he said.
All government organs not already [in] Canton, including Cabinet and Legislative Yuan, will move here immediately. Acting President expected possibly next week.

Despite forceful manner in which Wu expressed determination stop Communists, he admitted they had already crossed Yangtze in two places—one west of Wuhu, the other up river from Chinkiang, at both of which places serious fighting, he said, is now taking place.
